titleStmt.title: Letter from William Johnson to the chief secretary, 14 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Johnson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is from William Johnson, a Newry based solicitor, and is addressed to the chief-secretary. Augustine Birrell (1850-1933), was the chief secretary for Ireland from 1907 until 1916. He resigned as chief-secretary in the aftermath of the Easter Rising, for which he accepted responsibility.In the letter, Johnson writes on behalf of the Executors of Mrs. Mary Campbell, of Warrenpoint County Down, deceased. Mrs. Campbell was interred in a grave thought to be that of her late husband but her husband’s relatives have objected to her being interred there. Thus the Executors want to have the remains exhumed and re-interred in a different plot and they require the Chief Secretary’s approval.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6717

correspAction.placeName: Marcus Square, Newry, County Down, Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
6 Marqus Square Newry 14th April, 1916 Dear Sir, Mary Campbell late of Warrenpointin the County
of Down, Widow, deceased. The aboveânamed deceased died at Warrenpoint on the 16th Februaâ
ry 1916 and I act for her Executors â Mr. James Kelly, Newry St. Warrenâ
point, Master Carter and Mr. John Crawford, The Square, Warrenpoint, Merâ
chant â to whom the Probate of the Will of deceased has been granted. Mrs. Mary Campbell was interred in the Roman Catholic Buryingâ
ground attached to the Burren Roman Catholic Church and situate in the
Townland of Burren. She was interred in the grave supposed to belong
to her late Husband, but it appears the grave in which she was interred
is claimed by some of the relatives of her late Husband and they obâ
ject to the body remaining there. The Executors, under the circumstances, do not wish any unpleasantâ
ness about the matter and they are desirous of lifting the coffin and
having the coffin with the remains interred in fresh ground in the
Burren Roman Catholic Graveâyard, which has been allocated to them for
that purpose. Will you be good enough to make an Order giving the Executors
power to take the coffin with the body out of the existing grave and
put it in a new grave in the same graveâyard. My authority for making this request is O'Connor's Justice of
the Peace, last Edition, Page 886 Note 4. Yours faithfully, William Johnson The Chief Secretary, Lord Lieutenant Dublin Castle, DUBLIN.
